evals.prompt: I have a React app with a long dropdown menu (like 50+ options) and I want to add those nice gradient fades at the top and bottom like you see in iOS apps, so users know there's more content to scroll. The dropdown has a white background and max height of 320px. Can you help me implement this?
evals.expected_output: Complete implementation including: (1) CSS with wrapper pattern and white background variant, (2) React useScrollFade hook with TypeScript, (3) Integration example showing how to wrap the dropdown, (4) Explanation of the wrapper pattern and why it's needed

assertions.name: uses_wrapper_pattern
assertions.description: Implementation uses the wrapper pattern (parent holds masks via pseudo-elements, child scrolls) rather than conditional rendering of fade divs

assertions.name: provides_reusable_hook
assertions.description: Provides a reusable useScrollFade hook that can be imported and used in other components, not just inline scroll logic

assertions.name: includes_resize_observer
assertions.description: Uses ResizeObserver to handle dynamic content height changes, not just scroll events

assertions.name: uses_data_attributes
assertions.description: Uses data-fade-top and data-fade-bottom attributes to control mask visibility via CSS, not inline styles or conditional rendering

assertions.name: multi_stop_gradient
assertions.description: Gradient uses multiple opacity stops (6+ stops) for smooth fade, not just a simple two-color gradient

assertions.name: white_background_variant
assertions.description: Explicitly uses or mentions fc-scroll-fade-wrapper-white class for white backgrounds

evals.prompt: hey so i added scroll fade masks to my vue 3 sidebar but now i can't scroll at all?? the content is there but it won't move when i try to scroll. here's what i did: i wrapped my sidebar-list div with the fc-scroll-fade-wrapper and added the data attributes but scrolling just doesn't work. what am i doing wrong
evals.expected_output: Troubleshooting response that: (1) Identifies likely causes (overflow: hidden on wrapper, missing overflow-y: auto on content, height constraints), (2) Provides debug checklist with console commands, (3) Shows correct CSS structure with conditional rules, (4) Explains the wrapper should NOT have overflow properties

assertions.name: identifies_overflow_hidden_issue
assertions.description: Explicitly identifies that overflow: hidden on the wrapper is a common cause of the scrolling problem

assertions.name: explains_wrapper_no_overflow
assertions.description: Clearly states that the wrapper should NOT have overflow properties - the inner content element should have overflow-y: auto

assertions.name: provides_correct_architecture
assertions.description: Shows the correct two-layer architecture: wrapper (position: relative, no overflow) + inner content (overflow-y: auto, height constraint)

assertions.name: includes_vue_composable
assertions.description: Provides Vue 3 Composition API code (ref, onMounted, onUnmounted) for scroll detection, not just CSS fixes

assertions.name: mentions_height_constraint
assertions.description: Mentions that the scroll container needs a height constraint (height: 100%, max-height, or flex: 1)

assertions.name: no_incorrect_wrapper_scroll
assertions.description: Does NOT incorrectly recommend putting overflow-y: auto on the wrapper itself (this breaks the pseudo-element mask pattern)

evals.prompt: I'm building a chat interface in vanilla JavaScript (no frameworks) and want to add scroll fade indicators at the top/bottom of the message list. The background is a light beige color (#f5f2ef). The messages are in a div with class 'chat-messages' that's inside a flex container. How do I implement this without React or Vue?
evals.expected_output: Complete vanilla JS implementation including: (1) CSS with custom beige color variant or guidance to use fc-scroll-fade-wrapper-warm, (2) ScrollFadeMask class with init/destroy methods, (3) Usage example showing how to instantiate, (4) HTML structure with wrapper pattern, (5) Explanation of how to integrate with existing flex layout

assertions.name: provides_vanilla_js_class
assertions.description: Provides a JavaScript class (not React/Vue) that can be instantiated with new ScrollFadeMask(element)

assertions.name: custom_background_color
assertions.description: Either provides CSS with #f5f2ef background color in gradients, or mentions fc-scroll-fade-wrapper-warm class, or explains how to customize gradient colors

assertions.name: includes_init_destroy
assertions.description: Class has proper lifecycle methods (init/constructor and destroy/cleanup) for adding/removing event listeners

assertions.name: uses_wrapper_pattern_vanilla
assertions.description: Uses the wrapper pattern with CSS classes and data attributes, not inline style manipulation

assertions.name: resize_observer_vanilla
assertions.description: Uses ResizeObserver in the vanilla JS implementation for dynamic content changes

assertions.name: flex_layout_guidance
assertions.description: Mentions or shows how to integrate with flex containers (min-height: 0, flex: 1, etc.)
